And as always, thank you for the opportunity to present to the committee on a topic of importance for educators, students, and families across New Mexico. School climate. Joining me today are Deputy Secretary Yvonne Garcia, Deputy Secretary of Student Support Services at the Public Education Department and Dr. Sonia Siz, Behavioral Health Manager for PED Safe and Healthy Schools Bureau. Now, the brief you see in front of you discusses school climate as a concept examines existing data on New Mexico students perceptions of their educational experience and offers an analysis of early indications from the state's school climate survey results. In addition, the brief provides an overview of the roles of school counselors, social workers, and psychologists, brief key behavioral health roles involved in supporting student well-being and highlight strategies seem nationwide to recruit and retain these professionals. Now, I know that school climate can sometimes feel like a nebulous concept. And when you ask folks to describe how to tell if a school has a positive or healthy climate, it's common to hear some variation of this.
